title: Why Modern History Matters
body:
Modern history matters because the institutions we live inside were built in this period. Constitutions, bureaucracies, welfare systems, and global finance are all modern creations.

It also matters because many present conflicts are historical in disguise. Debates about borders, identity, reparations, migration, and trade all carry the weight of recent history.

Without modern history, people mistake inherited structures for natural ones. History reveals that the current order was made, and therefore can be unmade or redesigned.

That makes modern history one of the most practical humanities subjects there is.
keyPoints: Many current conflicts are legacy conflicts
History helps distinguish contingency from necessity
Memory and identity are political forces
Understanding the past improves judgment in the present
furtherReading: The Age of Empire by Eric Hobsbawm
Why Nations Fail by Daron Acemoglu and James Robinson
